Title: Revealing evolution of tropane alkaloid biosynthesis by analyzing two genomes in the Solanaceae family

Common_Genes.Applied_Species: Atropa belladonna; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Atropa belladonna; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes norhyoscyamine accumulation by catalyzing N-demethylation of hyoscyamine.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EVM0022661.2 was identified from the CYP82M clade and is highly coexpressed with AbCYP82M3 genes in primary and secondary roots. 2. Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana and yeast microsome assays converted hyoscyamine to norhyoscyamine. 3. Overexpression in A. belladonna hairy roots increased norhyoscyamine ~2.09- to 5.70-fold, while CRISPR editing reduced it to ~17% to ~26% of control.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Predominantly expressed/coexpressed in primary and secondary roots
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Predominantly associated with primary and secondary roots in A. belladonna
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Coexpression analysis; tobacco transient expression assay; yeast microsome enzyme assay; overexpression in hairy roots; CRISPR/Cas9 editing; metabolite profiling by UPLC-MS
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse growth; hyoscyamine substrate infiltration in tobacco leaves; yeast microsome assay with hyoscyamine
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (NCBI SRA PRJNA766188 for Atropa belladonna)
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper notes only trace norhyoscyamine was detected in transient tobacco and yeast assays, suggesting relatively weak or potentially nonspecific N-demethylation activity; authors discuss possible convergent evolution with human CYPs.
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter in pBI121 overexpression construct; CRISPR/Cas9 construct promoter not specified

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: 4-methylaminobutanal / oxidatively deaminated intermediate leading to tropinone
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes oxidative deamination of N-methylputrescine in an early key step of tropane alkaloid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies early pathway intermediate required for hyoscyamine and scopolamine formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes hyoscyamine and scopolamine accumulation; RNAi suppression significantly decreases both metabolites in hairy roots.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AbMPO1 was identified as one of two cloned MPO candidates in A. belladonna. 2. It is highly expressed in roots. 3. RNAi suppression of AbMPO1 significantly reduced hyoscyamine and scopolamine contents, establishing it as the primary functional MPO for mTA biosynthesis in A. belladonna.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene cloning; expression analysis; RNA interference in hairy roots; HPLC metabolite quantification
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Hairy root culture under greenhouse-derived material
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (NCBI SRA PRJNA766188 for Atropa belladonna)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AbMPO1, not AbMPO2, is the primary functional MPO in A. belladonna.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Putative MPO homolog tested for involvement in oxidative deamination of N-methylputrescine.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Candidate early-step enzyme but not supported as major contributor to end-product formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: AbMPO2 suppression did not significantly affect hyoscyamine or scopolamine levels in hairy roots.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AbMPO2 is a second MPO homolog cloned from A. belladonna. 2. It is predominantly expressed in aboveground tissues. 3. RNAi suppression did not significantly reduce hyoscyamine or scopolamine, indicating it is not the primary MPO for mTA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene cloning; expression analysis; RNA interference in hairy roots; HPLC metabolite quantification
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (NCBI SRA PRJNA766188 for Atropa belladonna)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a tested core candidate because the study experimentally distinguished it from AbMPO1.

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Atropa belladonna; Datura stramonium; Capsicum annuum; Solanum lycopersicum; Solanum tuberosum
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Atropa belladonna; Datura stramonium; Capsicum annuum; Solanum lycopersicum; Solanum tuberosum
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Atropa belladonna; Datura stramonium; Capsicum annuum; Solanum lycopersicum; Solanum tuberosum
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Atropa belladonna; Datura stramonium; Capsicum annuum; Solanum lycopersicum; Solanum tuberosum
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces tropinone to tropine using NADPH.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms the core tropane scaffold tropine required for downstream TA and mTA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream within module I / terminal step of tropine formation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point supported for tropine distribution; duplication-associated control
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functional TRIs with higher catalytic efficiency and root expression promote tropine formation and are associated with TA-producing species; low-efficiency TRI in tomato helps explain lack of detectable tropine.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TRI was functionally identified in A. belladonna and compared across multiple Solanaceae species. 2. TRI and CYP82M3 form a conserved gene cluster across Solanaceae. 3. TRI duplication and subfunctionalization are associated with higher root expression, stronger enzymatic activity, and tropine accumulation in TA-producing species. 4. SlTRI has much lower catalytic efficiency than AbTRI, DsTRI, and CaTRI. 5. L159V substitution increased SlTRI activity and restored activity to StTRI mutant.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Duplicated TRI genes show diverse expression; at least one TRI is highly expressed in roots of TA-producing species
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in roots in TA-producing species
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots, stems, and leaves were tested; tropine detected in A. belladonna, D. stramonium, and C. annuum
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro enzyme assay; kinetic analysis; site-directed mutagenesis; metabolite profiling in plants; phylogeny; microsynteny
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse growth; enzyme assays with NADPH and tropinone
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential toxicological concerns from tropine in edible pepper discussed; no direct growth penalty reported
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: TRI duplication rather than mere cluster presence is linked to robust tropine production; retained TRIs in tomato/potato may be pseudogenizing or neofunctionalized.

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Atropa belladonna; Capsicum annuum; Solanum lycopersicum
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Atropa belladonna; Capsicum annuum; Solanum lycopersicum
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes formation of tropinone from 4-(1-methyl-2-pyrrodinyl)-3-oxobutanoic acid.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms the immediate precursor of tropine and thereby supports TA/mTA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Conserved CYP82M3 activity across Solanaceae supports tropinone production; absence of tropine in tomato is not due to loss of CYP82M3 activity.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP82M3 and TRI form a conserved gene cluster across Solanaceae. 2. CaCYP82M3 and SlCYP82M3 produced tropinone when coexpressed with AbPMT and AbPYKS in tobacco leaves. 3. CYP82M3 genes are predominantly expressed in roots across species examined. 4. Conserved CYP82M3 activity indicates TRI divergence is more important than CYP82M3 loss for tropine absence in tomato.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Predominantly expressed in roots in examined Solanaceae species; assayed in tobacco leaves by transient expression
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient coexpression in tobacco leaves with AbPMT and AbPYKS; UPLC-MS detection of tropinone; phylogeny; microsynteny
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana greenhouse-grown leaves
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Single-copy within the cluster across examined Solanaceae, unlike TRI which frequently duplicated.

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Petunia axillaris; Atropa belladonna; Datura stramonium; Capsicum annuum; Solanum lycopersicum
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Petunia axillaris; Atropa belladonna; Datura stramonium; Capsicum annuum; Solanum lycopersicum
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Petunia axillaris; Atropa belladonna; Datura stramonium; Capsicum annuum; Solanum lycopersicum
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Petunia axillaris; Atropa belladonna; Datura stramonium; Capsicum annuum; Solanum lycopersicum
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces phenylpyruvic acid to phenyllactic acid using NADPH.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms precursor for phenyllactylglucose and ultimately littorine/hyoscyamine/scopolamine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not a bottleneck according to the study
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PPAR activity is conserved across Solanaceae, indicating broad capacity to produce phenyllactic acid precursor rather than being a limiting step for mTA specialization.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PPAR homologs from five Solanaceae species all converted phenylpyruvic acid to phenyllactic acid in vitro. 2. The enzyme is broadly conserved across Solanaceae. 3. This supports the conclusion that PPAR is unlikely to be the bottleneck for phenyllactylglucose biosynthesis or mTA specialization.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro recombinant enzyme assays; UPLC-MS product detection
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Enzyme assays with phenylpyruvic acid and NADPH
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq support for comparative pathway analysis (PRJNA766188; PRJNA765895)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because the paper experimentally validates conserved activity across species as part of pathway evolution analysis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Lower-priority engineering target because activity is widespread and likely not limiting; useful as pathway support gene.

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Atropa belladonna; Datura stramonium; Lycium chinense
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Atropa belladonna; Datura stramonium; Lycium chinense
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes esterification of tropine with phenyllactylglucose-derived tropyl moiety to form littorine.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms littorine, the immediate intermediate leading to hyoscyamine and scopolamine
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functional LS exists in A. belladonna and D. stramonium but not tested Lycium homolog; enables littorine production and contributes to restricted mTA distribution.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DsLS and AbLS produced littorine in tobacco leaves when coexpressed with AbUGT1 and supplied substrates, whereas LcLS did not. 2. DsLS showed significantly higher activity than AbLS. 3. Comparative synteny and sequence analysis suggest LS was lost in non-mTA-producing Solanaceae lineages.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Associated with mTA-producing species; pathway genes highly expressed in roots
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Assayed by transient expression in tobacco leaves
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Littorine detected in A. belladonna and D. stramonium
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression assay in tobacco; substrate feeding; UPLC-MS; comparative phylogeny and microsynteny
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Substrate infiltration with tropine and phenyllactate; coexpression with AbUGT1
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq comparative expression (PRJNA766188; PRJNA765895); L. chinense full-length transcriptome PRJNA769498
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study emphasizes gene loss of LS in non-mTA-producing species as a basis for restricted mTA distribution.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value engineering target for reconstructing the mTA branch; DsLS may be superior to AbLS for heterologous production.
